rules:
Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Search your deck for a Fighting Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Amaura or Tyrunt and put them onto your bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

This card can only be attached to Fighting Pokémon. This card provides Fighting Energy only while this card is attached to a Fighting Pokémon.

The attacks of the Fighting Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

(If this card is attached to anything other than a Fighting Pokémon, discard this card.)

This card can only be attached to Grass Pokémon. This card provides Grass Energy only while this card is attached to a Grass Pokémon.

When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Grass Pokémon, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

(If this card is attached to anything other than a Grass Pokémon, discard this card.)

Each Stage 1 and Stage 2 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +30 HP.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to your Pokémon and put them into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Pokémon this card is attached to can't be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting that Pokémon.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Amaura you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Prevent all damage done to Benched Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. (If you can't shuffle 2 cards into your deck, you can't play this card.) Then, draw a card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for a Fighting Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Tyrunt you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Amaura or Tyrunt and put them onto your bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Fighting Pokémon this card is attached to has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, that Pokémon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead. Then, discard this card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

The attacks of each Fighting Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) do 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

flavorText: They live in pitch black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Twister Throw
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

attacks.name: Held-Item Inspection
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Choose an Item card you find there. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Thorough Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

attacks.name: Safety Check
attacks.effect: Look at 1 of your face-down Prize cards.

flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Knuckle Sandwich
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The world's laziest Pokémon. It moves to another spot when there's no food left within its reach.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its heartbeat is fast and its blood so agitated that it can't sit still for one second.

attacks.name: Big Yawn
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.
A rare Pokémon that adapts to harsh environments by taking on different evolutionary forms.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Lickichop
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its long tongue, slathered with a gooey saliva, sticks to anything, so it is very useful.

attacks.name: Boomburst
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. Nothing can beat them in a battle in the dark.

attacks.name: Rainbow Shower
attacks.effect: Attach as many basic Energy cards as you like from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Sand Sweep
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

flavorText: It hides itself by kicking up desert sand with its wings. Red covers shield its eyes from sand.

attacks.name: Charge Energy
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

attacks.name: Jet Sonic
attacks.effect: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fairy Lock
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

attacks.name: Curly Ribbon
attacks.effect: Move an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights.

attacks.name: Follow Me
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its hearing is so acute it can hear a pin drop over half a mile away. It lives on quiet mountains.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

attacks.name: Moonlight
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

attacks.name: Clobber
attacks.effect: You may discard an Item card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won't put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.

attacks.name: Machine Gun Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is.
Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling it skin up to its neck.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It takes great pride in its strength. Even though it can tear foes apart, it finishes them off with powerful poison.

attacks.name: Moonsault Stomp
attacks.effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flying Press
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't a Pokémon-EX, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama.

attacks.name: Chew Up
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Jaw Fossil
flavorText: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn't like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

attacks.name: Bad-Influence Evolution
attacks.effect: If you have any Darkness Pokémon on your Bench, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

attacks.name: Shout of Power
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as "The Guardian of the Fields."

attacks.name: Aero Turn
attacks.effect: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: It wields the fur on its arm like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It takes pride in the speed at which it can use moves. What it loses in power, it makes up for in quantity.

attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It lives in arid deserts. It makes a sloping pit trap in sand where it patiently awaits prey.
It stomps on the ground to build power. It can send a 10-ton truck flying with a straight-arm punch.
It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over. It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Helicoptero
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It fights while spinning like a top. The centrifugal force boosts its destructive power by 10.

attacks.name: Bullet Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

attacks.name: Machamp Crush
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its four ruggedly developed arms can launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds.
Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.
It hefts a Graveler repeatedly to strengthen its entire body. It uses every type of martial arts.

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 60 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

flavorText: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

attacks.name: Psy Report
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

attacks.name: Fortunate Eye
attacks.effect: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back on top of his or her deck in any order.

flavorText: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

attacks.name: Hug
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.

attacks.name: Hand Control
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. You may choose a Supporter card you find there. If you do, your opponent plays that Supporter card. However, you make all decisions for that card. (That Supporter card is discarded.)

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It carries a pendulum-like device. There once was an incident in which it took away a child it hypnotized.

attacks.name: Sinister Suggestion
attacks.effect: Whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her next turn, treat it as tails.

flavorText: Puts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating only bad dreams.

attacks.name: Entrainment
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Energy Short
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

attacks.name: Raging Thunder Punch
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Lightning Slam
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Lightning Slam during your next turn.

flavorText: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Negative Discard
attacks.effect: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

attacks.name: Positive Hand
attacks.effect: Discard a basic Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Electric Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

attacks.name: Tag Team Spark
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your Magmortar.

attacks.name: Gigavolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its body glows a whitish blue.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The diamond-shaped crystals on its body expel air as cold as -240 degrees Fahrenheit, surrounding its enemies and encasing them in ice.

flavorText: This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years.

attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Their enormous claws launch cannonballs of water powerful enough to pierce tanker hulls.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol.

attacks.name: Igloo Hold
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Mountain Drop
attacks.effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: They love the cold seas of the north. They create pathways across the ocean waters by freezing their own breath.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its nose is always running. It sniffs the snot back up because the mucus provides the raw material for its moves.

attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: By controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.

attacks.name: Steamroll
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: With its extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.

attacks.name: Finishing Blow
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It can live in or out of water. When out of water, it constantly sweats to keep its body slimy.

attacks.name: Spiral Current
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. That Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It has a flame sac inside its belly that perpetually burns. It feels warm if it is hugged.

flavorText: Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you'll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.
It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

flavorText: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.
